Amended Notice Of Motion For Relief From Default
Off Calendar Jun-24-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RE: DEFENDANT KAREN FITZPATRICK's, EXECUTOR OF THE ESTATE OF PHILIP RICHARD WATT Amended Notice Of Motion For Relief From Default.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person.

The court adopts its tentative ruling. The defense motion for relief from default is granted.

Robert Baldwin and Philip Watt had a romantic relationship. On June 20, 2021, they had a physical altercation. Baldwin sued Watt; he filed for entry of default on November 2, 2022. No default judgment has been filed. Watt died of a drug overdose on October 10, 2023. Watt's sister Karen Fitzpatrick - appointed executor of his estate on February 24, 2024 - filed the pending motion.

A "court has inherent, equitable power to set aside a judgment on the ground of extrinsic fraud or mistake" - a broad concept that applies if a party is mentally incompetent when default judgment is entered. (Edmon & Karnow, California Practice Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial (TRG 2016) 5:435, 5:452, citing Kulchar v. Kulchar (1969) 1 Cal.3d 467, 471-72.) The same must apply where, as here, there is entry of default, but not yet judgment. Four declarants who observed Watt's mental state in the relevant period convincingly show incompetence. Moreover, California law strongly favors adjudication of disputes on their merits.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr. Clerk: R. Woods. Not Reported. (302/RBU)

Motion For Relief From Default
Off Calendar Jun-06-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: There were no appearances at the initial calling of the case.

Appearances via Zoom:
2nd "calling" of the case: Thomas W. Purtell, Esq., Tel. 415.722.6291, appearing for plaintiff Robert Baldwin;

3rd "calling" of the case: Trang La, Esq., Tel. 415.626.7744, appearing for defendant Karen Fitzpatrick, Executor of the Estate of Philip Richard Watt.

Hearing re: DEFENDANT KAREN FITZPATRICK, EXECUTOR OF THE ESTATE OF PHILIP RICHARD WATT'S Motion For Relief From Default.

This matter was not contested. At the initial calling of the matter, the tentative ruling was adopted as follows:

Ordered off calendar for movant's noncompliance with Local Rule 2.7(B) (courtesy copies). Movant may re-set the motion for a Mon.-Thur. after June 20. Movant's papers shall bear new hearing date. Plaintiff's opposition papers complied with Rule 2.7(B) so they need not be re-filed.

2nd call: Mr. Purtell stated an appearance. No other appearances. The Court confirmed the tentative ruling.

3rd call: Ms. La stated an appearance. Mr. Purtell was not present. Ms. La stated that she was not contesting the tentative ruling only seeking clarification. Brief discussions are held. Ms. La is to contact the other side and re-set the motion.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: Malone. Matter not reported. (302/RBU)
